title: The Toolkit - Week 2
subject: Claude vs ChatGPT for work: we tested both for a week
preheader: Plus: Copy.ai and Jasper go head-to-head on marketing copy
bodyHtml:
The Toolkit

AI Productivity Hub · Week of April 14, 2026

This week we put the two biggest AI assistants through a full workweek of real tasks — and tested the leading AI copywriting tools side by side. The results surprised us.

ChatGPT — The Speed King

For quick-turnaround tasks — drafting emails, summarizing reports, brainstorming names — ChatGPT is still the fastest path from question to answer. GPT-4o’s multimodal features shine when you need to analyze screenshots or charts. Where it falls short: longer documents lose coherence, and it still hallucinates citations. Best for: speed-critical tasks and visual analysis. Read our full review →

Claude — The Deep Thinker

Claude excels where ChatGPT struggles: long-form analysis, nuanced writing, and following complex multi-step instructions. We gave both a 40-page contract to summarize — Claude caught three key risks ChatGPT missed entirely. It’s also noticeably better at saying “I don’t know.” Best for: research, analysis, and anything requiring careful reasoning. Read our full review →

Copy.ai — Marketing Copy at Scale

Copy.ai has evolved well beyond a tagline generator. Their workflow feature lets you chain prompts together — feed in a product brief and get blog outlines, ad copy, and social posts in one pass. The output quality is solid B+ work that needs a human polish, not a full rewrite. Verdict: best value for marketing teams producing high volume content. Read our full review →

Jasper — Enterprise-Ready but Pricey

Jasper’s brand voice feature is genuinely impressive — train it on your existing content and it maintains consistent tone across all outputs. The problem: at $49/mo for the Creator plan, it’s 3x the cost of Copy.ai for similar core features. The enterprise-tier collaboration and approval workflows justify the price for larger teams, but solos should look elsewhere. Verdict: worth it for teams of 5+; overkill for individuals. Read our full review →

Head-to-Head

ChatGPT vs Claude: The Full Comparison

We scored both assistants across 10 real business tasks: email drafting, code review, data analysis, meeting prep, and more. See the full scorecard with detailed results.

See the Full Comparison →

That’s Week 2. Next week we’re testing AI scheduling tools — stay tuned. Reply with any tool requests.
